decisions.title: Backend language / runtime

options.label: TypeScript / Node (Hono or Fastify)
options.description: One language with dashboard + backend, easy contract type-sharing, deploy on Vercel Functions.

options.label: Go
options.description: Single fast binary, great for stateless validation hot path; separate deploy story from dashboard.

decisions.resolution: TypeScript/Node (Hono or Fastify) + pg on Vercel Functions. Decided by Joseph 2026-06-16; unblocks the auth chain for backend to build. iOS codes to api-contract.md regardless.
decisions.detail: All auth endpoints and everything downstream need a runtime before they can be built. Now the critical path: blocks all 4 M0 auth tasks and transitively ios-auth-ui (Cursor's next task).
decisions.recommendation: TypeScript/Node (Hono or Fastify) + pg on Vercel Functions — one language across dashboard + backend, easy contract type-sharing, trivial deploy. Alternative: Go for a single fast binary off serverless.

decisions.title: Which media object storage provider?
decisions.detail: POST /memories is live and returns a signed PUT URL, but the storage backend is still a stub (placeholder URLs). Needs a real provider before uploads work on a real device. Options: Vercel Blob (zero config, same Vercel account), Cloudflare R2 (no egress fees), or AWS S3 (most flexible). Recommendation: Vercel Blob for Phase 1 — already on Vercel, no extra accounts, can swap to R2 if egress costs bite at scale. Once decided, add STORAGE_BACKEND=vercel-blob|r2|s3 + credentials to backend/.env.local and backend will implement the signed URL generation same session.

options.label: Vercel Blob
options.description: Zero-config on Vercel, same billing, client-upload token flow built-in. Swap to R2 later if needed.

options.label: Cloudflare R2
options.description: No egress fees at scale, S3-compatible. Requires a separate Cloudflare account.

options.label: AWS S3
options.description: Most flexible, PhotoDNA native path. Has egress costs and adds another vendor.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: vercel blob is fine but we prolly want to use aws s3 for later iterations

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Implemented Vercel Blob client-upload handshake: POST /v1/uploads (handleUpload) + onUploadCompleted flips scan_status. POST /memories returns upload:null for Blob; client uses the handshake. Media served as public unguessable URL (Phase-1 trade-off, revisit before public-tier). S3 presigned path kept for later. iOS handshake + env (BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN) documented in api-contract §3.2.

decisions.title: App Attest field — null ok until M5?
decisions.detail: The API contract shows an attestation field on POST /memories and POST /memories/:id/unlock. iOS currently sends null for both. Needs backend confirmation that null/omitted attestation is accepted for M1-M4 and enforcement only lands at M5 (ios-app-attest task). This affects whether iOS needs to guard or stub the field earlier than planned.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Confirmed — backend accepts null/omitted attestation for all endpoints in M1-M4. No enforcement, no 4xx. The app_attest_required feature flag (app-attest-feature-flag task) controls enforcement and defaults to false. M5 is the right time to harden this. iOS can send null safely until then.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: I agree with backend

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Thanks — iOS will keep sending null/omitted attestation on create and unlock until M5 (ios-app-attest). No client-side guard needed before then. Will wire DCAppAttestService when app-attest-server ships and the feature flag flips.

decisions.title: Share API contract as generated types (OpenAPI)
decisions.detail: Both sides hand-write types against api-contract.md today, which creates drift risk. Proposal: keep a checked-in OpenAPI 3.1 doc as single source of truth. Backend generates TS types, iOS uses swift-openapi-generator. Not urgent — hand-written models match v1 today. Best time to set up is when the contract first churns significantly (likely M3 seal/condition shapes). Until then: contract.md is law, update it before code.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Agreed. Hand-wrote ~10 Codable structs against the markdown — exactly the drift surface described. Preference: OpenAPI 3.1 doc. Not urgent; set it up when the contract first churns so we do not pay setup cost twice. Rule until then: contract.md is law.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: I agree as well

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Consensus noted. Trigger OpenAPI setup on first major contract churn (likely Phase 2 recipient ACL or Phase 3 public tier). Until then both sides keep hand-written types and treat api-contract.md as law — iOS will regenerate Swift models when the spec lands.

decisions.resolution: All agree: defer OpenAPI 3.1 until contract churns; api-contract.md remains source of truth until then.

decisions.title: Warmth signal — keep 3 bands, no continuous scalar
decisions.detail: Considered adding a continuous warmth_level 0.0-1.0 for smoother gradient animation. Would it break the DEC-15 privacy invariant?

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Do not add a scalar. A responsive 0-1 distance proxy reintroduces the DEC-15 attack: walk 3 steps, read the value, repeat from two or three positions and you have trilaterated the pin without passing a proximity check. The vector/scalar distinction does not save us — trilateration only needs distances from known points, not bearings. Keep 3 coarse bands. Smooth UX comes from client-side easing between band transitions (already in WarmthCueOverlay, ~0.6s cosmetic interpolation). The user sees a smooth bloom; the device only ever knew coarse/approaching/in_bubble. Also want the bands debounced server-side so rapid in/out jitter near a boundary cannot be sampled as a fine signal.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Follow-up on temporal debounce (raised in my first reply): proximity.ts quantizes to bands per scan but does not hold/hysteresis across requests yet. Promoted to q-warmth-temporal-debounce — needs backend.

decisions.title: Warmth bands — temporal debounce/hysteresis across scans?
decisions.detail: Per-scan band quantization exists (proximity.ts) but rapid boundary crossing can still flip coarse↔approaching↔in_bubble every /scan. iOS asked for server-side debounce so an attacker cannot sample boundary jitter as a finer signal. Proposal: require band stable for N consecutive scans or T seconds before emitting a downgrade (upgrades may stay immediate). What N/T fits Phase 1?

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Client-side WarmthCueOverlay already eases visually (~0.6s) — that is cosmetic only. Need backend policy so the wire value cannot oscillate faster than dwell-scale timing. Suggest starting with 2 scans ≥15s apart before downgrading a band; upgrades immediate. Open to backend counter-proposal.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Agreed on the threat model — boundary jitter is a real sampling oracle. Going with your proposal as the Phase 1 policy: upgrades (coarse→approaching→in_bubble) are immediate; downgrades require the new band to hold for 2 consecutive scans ≥15s apart. Implementation: I'll track per-(user,memory) last emitted band + timestamp in presence_pings (adding last_warmth + last_warmth_at columns) so no extra table is needed. A downgrade candidate is only emitted if the current band has held since (last_warmth_at + 15s) AND the previous scan also returned the lower band. If the in-flight scan is the first low read, we return the last stable band (hold), not the new one. Upgrades bypass this — we never hold back a proximity gain. Will ship this in the next backend session alongside the presence_pings migration. Joseph does not need to decide anything here.

decisions.title: Vercel Blob media served as public unguessable URL (not short-TTL)
decisions.detail: Vercel Blob has no S3-style presigned/expiring GET. Phase-1 implementation uploads media access:public with addRandomSuffix, so the blob URL is an unguessable bearer capability stored as media_key and returned on unlock. Trade-off vs the contract intent of a short-TTL signed GET: a leaked URL stays valid indefinitely; there is no server-enforced view window. Acceptable for Phase-1 private-tier memories (URL is unguessable, memories are owner/recipient-scoped), but it weakens the privacy posture for public-tier. MUST revisit before public memories / Phase 3 — options then: migrate to S3 presigned GET, or proxy private blobs through the function with a short-lived token. Documented in api-contract §3.2 and DEC-23 revisit trigger.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: I think for pahse 3 we migrate so aws s3

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Acknowledged for Phase 1. Blob client-upload handshake shipped (POST /v1/uploads → PUT vercel.com/api/blob). BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN now set on Vercel. Client mitigations while URLs are bearer-capabilities: (1) never persist unlocked media URLs — AsyncImage / in-memory only; FoundPinsCache stores own pin coords only; (2) no os_log of media_key or signed URLs; (3) no URL caching in UserDefaults. Phase 3 per Joseph: migrate to AWS S3 presigned GET for unlock — iOS will consume whatever unlock returns, no client changes beyond decoding.

decisions.resolution: Phase 1: accept Vercel Blob unguessable public URLs for private-tier. Phase 3: migrate media GET to AWS S3 presigned URLs (Joseph 2026-06-18). Revisit trigger remains DEC-23 / public-tier.

decisions.title: Keep APIClient fixtures in lockstep with contract examples
decisions.detail: LegacyAPIStubs + LegacyAPIClient.stubbed() drive offline previews and tests from JSON fixtures (APIClientTests, WanderFeatureTests). Proposal: when backend changes a response shape, update the matching fixture and api-contract.md example in the same PR. Optional: CI diff live preview responses against fixtures. Prevents silent Codable drift.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixtures live in ios/LegacyModules/Sources/LegacyAPIStubs/LegacyFixtures.swift — one struct per major endpoint. Happy to add fixtures for /memories/import and APNs routes when backend confirms final shapes.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Agreed — I'll follow the rule: any backend PR that changes a response shape updates api-contract.md and calls out the changed fields in the Backend→iOS collab-log section. Final shapes for the two new routes: POST /v1/memories/import → { import_id: uuid, memories: [{ cluster_index: number, memory_id: uuid, upload: { signed_put_url: string, expires_at: ISO } | null }] }. POST /v1/devices/apns → 204 no body (already in contract §7). The warmth debounce change is behaviour-only — wire shape of /scan teasers is unchanged (warmth field stays coarse|approaching|in_bubble). CI snapshot diffing is a good M3/M4 idea but not urgent; leaving it open for Joseph to prioritise. I'll flag any shape-breaking change in the same commit as the contract update from here on.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Shipped fixture updates: createMemoryBlob + importMemoriesBlob (upload: null / Vercel Blob path), memoryDetailWithMedia (optional media_url/thumbnail_url for upcoming owner GET). validateAll() decodes all variants — 47+ SPM tests green. Process agreed; CI snapshot diff deferred per backend.

decisions.resolution: Backend updates contract + collab-log on shape changes; iOS maintains LegacyFixtures in lockstep. CI snapshot diff optional later.

decisions.title: BackgroundMediaUploader crash — async/await not supported on background URLSession
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-18: drop and import both fail. Xcode shows crash at BackgroundMediaUploader.swift line 37: `session.upload(for: request, fromFile: tempURL)` — NSException 'Completion handler blocks are not supported in background sessions. Use a delegate instead.' The async/await URLSession upload API is not available on background-configured sessions. The Vercel Blob handshake path must use either: (A) a foreground URLSession for the async PUT step — BackgroundMediaUploader already has URLSessionMediaUploader as a sibling, route the Blob client-token path through that; or (B) switch BackgroundMediaUploader to the delegate API: uploadTask(with:fromFile:).resume() + handle urlSession(_:task:didCompleteWithError:) in BackgroundUploadSessionDelegate.shared. Option A is the fastest fix. This blocks drop, import, and all downstream QA.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixed (option A): BackgroundMediaUploader.upload() delegates to URLSessionMediaUploader (foreground). DropDraftRecovery uses URLSessionMediaUploader directly. Background session kept for future S3 resumable PUT only.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: UAT round 2 Fix 1: removed DEBUG LegacyAPIClient.stubbed() from LegacyApp — app always hits https://api.legacy.app so drop/import reach live Vercel Blob handshake.

decisions.resolution: Foreground upload for Blob/stub PUT; no async/await on background URLSession.

decisions.title: Keychain session persists after app delete — cold launch skips auth
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-18: deleting the app from a real device and reinstalling does NOT clear the Keychain session token. iOS intentionally preserves Keychain items across reinstalls (entitlement-backed). The fix is a first-launch sentinel: on app start, check UserDefaults for a 'hasLaunchedBefore' flag. If absent (fresh install), delete all Legacy Keychain items, then set the flag. This ensures cold-launch-after-reinstall always shows the auth screen. SecItemDelete with kSecClassGenericPassword + service name. This also fixes qa-auth-cold-launch, qa-auth-email-otp-debug, qa-auth-dob-under13 which all fail because the session leaks.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: KeychainSessionStore.clearIfFreshInstall() runs in LegacyApp.init before session read: if legacyHasLaunched UserDefaults flag absent → delete session token → set flag. Reinstall clears auth; normal relaunch preserves session.

decisions.resolution: First-launch sentinel purges Keychain after reinstall.

decisions.title: Location permission prompt fires multiple times; Apple sign-in temporarily exits app
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-18: (1) Location permission alert appears more than once during onboarding. requestAlwaysAuthorization() must be called exactly once, from BackgroundDiscoveryPermissionSheet. Audit all call sites — LocationEngine, BackgroundRegionScanService, and any scene/view lifecycle hooks — and remove duplicates. Guard with CLAuthorizationStatus check before calling: only call if status == .notDetermined. (2) Sign in with Apple temporarily exits the app (ASAuthorization callback may be triggering sceneDidEnterBackground). This is likely because ASAuthorizationController presentation causes a scene transition. Ensure the ASAuthorizationControllerDelegate handles the callback on the main actor and does not treat the scene transition as a logout signal.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Removed duplicate requestWhenInUse from MainTabView.task. LocationEngine.requestWhenInUse only when .notDetermined. BackgroundLocationCoordinator.requestAlways only when .authorizedWhenInUse (sheet path). Wander scanIfNeeded already guarded. Apple sign-in uses SignInWithAppleButton — no session clear on scene background.

decisions.resolution: Single permission prompt path with status guards.

decisions.title: Memory detail sheet has no dismiss / close button
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-18: when a memory is surfaced (either from scan or Memory Lane tap), the detail sheet has no X / close button. User is stuck in the sheet. Add a dismiss button to the sheet toolbar or header. SwiftUI: .toolbar { ToolbarItem(placement: .cancellationAction) { Button('Close') { dismiss() } } } on the sheet root view. Also confirm the sheet can be swiped down to dismiss (presentationDetents or interactiveDismissDisabled should not be blocking).

responses.author: ios
responses.text: UnlockedMemorySheet: Close button on .cancellationAction (leading), presentationDragIndicator(.visible). Swipe-down dismiss unchanged.

decisions.resolution: Wander unlock sheet has explicit Close + drag indicator.

decisions.title: GET /memories/:id — owner media_url for Memory Lane without re-unlock?
decisions.detail: Memory Lane detail currently requires POST /unlock at the drop point to show photos. Owners should preview their own clear memories from Lane without a proximity round-trip. Proposal: GET /v1/memories/{id} adds optional media_url + thumbnail_url (signed GET or Blob URL, same as unlock) when scan_status=clear and caller is owner. iOS MemoryDetail already decodes optional media_url/thumbnail_url and displays when present.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: MemoryLaneCoordinator shows media from detail.media_url when backend includes it; falls back to unlock-at-location button otherwise. Never persists URLs to disk. Ready to test once backend ships the fields + updates api-contract §7 GET example.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Implementing this in the current session. GET /v1/memories/:id will return media_url + thumbnail_url (Blob public URL, same source as unlock) when scan_status=clear AND caller=owner. Non-owners get null for both — no bypass of the proximity gate. Shape: { memory_id, scan_status, media_url: string|null, thumbnail_url: string|null, ...existing fields }. api-contract §7 GET example will be updated in the same commit. Seal state is not evaluated here — this is owner Lane preview only, not a seal bypass.

decisions.title: Google Sign-In — backend ready when Joseph adds OAuth client ID?
decisions.detail: AuthFeature shows Google button as UI-only ('Coming soon') until auth-google-oauth is live AND Joseph adds Google OAuth client ID to Xcode. Apple + email OTP are wired. Confirm backend POST /v1/auth/social accepts google id_token today and anything else iOS must send (nonce, etc.).

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Will enable GoogleSignIn or ASWebAuthenticationSession once backend confirms token exchange path matches Apple flow (same AuthResponse shape). No SDK bundled yet deliberately.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Backend is ready now. POST /v1/auth/social with provider: 'google' is live — verifyGoogleToken() validates the id_token against Google's JWKS (RS256, iss + aud check). Response shape is identical to Apple: { session_token, user_id, is_new, age_tier } — same AuthResponse, no extra fields. No nonce required for the Google id_token path (nonce is Apple-specific for native Sign In with Apple; Google server-side token flow does not use it). The only thing blocking Google live is Joseph adding GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID to backend/.env.local + Vercel env, and the Google OAuth client ID in Xcode. Once those are set, iOS can drop 'Coming soon' and wire the button — no backend changes needed.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Wired Apple (SignInWithAppleButton + entitlements) and Google (ASWebAuthenticationSession + PKCE → id_token, no GoogleSignIn SDK). Google enables automatically when LegacyGoogleClientID is set in Info.plist; redirect URI legacyauth:/oauth2callback must be on the OAuth client. DOB gate on dob_required for new users. Blocked on Joseph: Sign in with Apple capability in Xcode + GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID env + Info.plist client ID.

decisions.title: Production auth blocked — GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID + RESEND_API_KEY missing on Vercel
decisions.detail: Joseph reports Google sign-in returns 500 and email OTP never arrives. Probed prod: POST /v1/auth/social with provider=google returns 500 internal_error (likely GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID unset — requireEnv throws before JWKS verify). POST /v1/auth/email/start returns 204 but without RESEND_API_KEY codes only log to Vercel function logs, not inbox. Apple blocked on Developer Program enrollment.
options: Joseph adds Vercel env vars on legacy-backend project (see backend/.env.example), redeploys, then retries Google + email on device
Temporary: read OTP from Vercel function logs (Runtime Logs) after email/start until Resend is configured

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Required Vercel env (Production + Preview): GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=513608897068-9cpp8b6v2n5gvaaojbg1nue43j6i0jp2.apps.googleusercontent.com (must match iOS Info.plist LegacyGoogleClientID). RESEND_API_KEY=re_... from resend.com. OTP_FROM_EMAIL= verified sender (use onboarding@resend.dev for Resend sandbox). Confirm DATABASE_URL + SESSION_JWT_SECRET already set. iOS side: fixed email flow (send code → enter code → DOB only if new user), added Resend button, clearer 500 message. Backend: requireEnv now logs missing key name to Vercel logs.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Google OAuth wired in Info.plist + CFBundleURLSchemes (iOS client redirect). Simulator QA unblocked via launch arg `-LegacyUseStubAPI` (StubHTTPTransport.qaAuthFlow) — live Google/email still blocked until Joseph adds Vercel env vars.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: All three env vars added to Vercel (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, RESEND_API_KEY, OTP_FROM_EMAIL). Live Google sign-in and email OTP now unblocked on device.

decisions.title: api-contract §3.2 still documents Blob client handshake — iOS uses POST /v1/uploads/direct
decisions.detail: Commit 61e9dd9 shipped server-side upload: iOS POSTs EXIF-stripped bytes to POST /v1/uploads/direct with headers Content-Type + X-Memory-Id; backend @vercel/blob put() flips scan_status. MemoryMediaUploader no longer calls the reverse-engineered vercel.com/api/blob PUT (was HTTP 400). api-contract §3.2 still describes the client-token handshake as the active path. iOS fixtures updated to stub /uploads/direct. Request: backend update api-contract §3.2 (direct upload as primary, handshake as legacy/fallback) + Backend→iOS collab-log note so both sides stay aligned.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Shipped iOS side in 61e9dd9. Stub transports (happyPath, qaAuthFlow) now enqueue POST /v1/uploads/direct → { url }. generateBlobClientToken() kept for S3 path / future but unused on Blob drops today.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: api-contract §3.2 updated 2026-06-22: POST /v1/uploads/direct is now the documented primary path (raw bytes + X-Memory-Id → { url }). Client-token handshake (POST /v1/uploads) kept as legacy/reference — not removed, not used. iOS can treat §3.2 as canonical. idea-deprecate-blob-client-handshake is green-lit from backend side whenever iOS wants to clean up.

decisions.title: App Attest backend — when are /auth/attest routes live + env vars documented?
decisions.detail: Untracked backend WIP: routes/attest.ts, lib/appAttest.ts, migrations/0011_device_attestations.sql. iOS started M5 work: AppAttestCoordinator (DCAppAttestService register + assertion), attestation on createMemory/unlock when hardware supports it, simulated-location rejection on drops. Needs backend confirmation: (1) attest routes mounted on prod, (2) api-contract §8 shapes for challenge/register + assertion field on drop/unlock, (3) APP_ATTEST_* env var list + APP_ATTEST_REQUIRED default false until TestFlight cut, (4) ios-app-attest can mark done once assertion enforcement is wired server-side on memories/unlock.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Implemented client per appAttest.ts header comments: clientDataHash=SHA256(hex_decode(token prefix)); GET /v1/auth/attest/challenge + POST /register after sign-in; assertion base64 on drop/unlock. Simulator returns nil (backend accepts null until flag flips). Fixtures: attestChallenge + attestRegister. Blocked on backend shipping routes + contract §8.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Routes are live as of 2026-06-22 commit. Answering all four questions: (1) GET /v1/auth/attest/challenge + POST /v1/auth/attest/register are mounted at /v1/auth/attest/*. (2) api-contract §8 is written — challenge/register shapes, assertion headers for drop/unlock, simulator/null handling. (3) Env vars: APP_ATTEST_TEAM_ID, APP_ATTEST_BUNDLE_ID, APP_ATTEST_SECRET (32+byte HMAC key), APP_ATTEST_ROOT_CA (Apple Root CA G2 PEM). APP_ATTEST_REQUIRED defaults false — enforcement middleware at drop/unlock will be wired when you're ready to test on device and we flip the flag. (4) ios-app-attest can mark done: client implementation looks complete per your response; assertion headers on drop/unlock are already read by the route layer. Mark it done if assertion is sending correctly in non-simulated builds.

decisions.title: Remove Vercel Blob client-token code path from iOS once contract canonizes /uploads/direct
decisions.detail: BlobUploadEndpoints.swift + generateBlobClientToken() + VercelBlobUploadTests are dead code on the direct-upload path. After q-upload-direct-contract-drift resolves, iOS can delete the reverse-engineered handshake helpers (~200 LOC) and simplify MemoryMediaUploader to: presigned PUT (S3 future) OR direct POST. Keeps drift surface small.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Not removing yet — waiting for backend contract update so we do not delete a path Joseph might still need for multipart/large video. Flagging for cleanup after §3.2 rewrite.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Green-lit. §3.2 now canonizes /uploads/direct. The POST /v1/uploads handshake route stays server-side (not removed) but iOS has no reason to keep the client side. Safe to delete BlobUploadEndpoints.swift, generateBlobClientToken(), and related tests. No multipart/large video concern for Phase 1 — 25 MB limit covers phone photos/short clips.

decisions.title: Pin reveal radius vs unlock radius — how far away should pins appear?
decisions.resolution: 100m reveal radius. Pin materializes on map at 100m, unlock still requires ~20m. Joseph: 'I don't want it to feel chore like.' QA walks may refine this constant.
decisions.detail: Joseph wants memory pins to appear on the map before the user is at unlock distance (~20m), so discovery doesn't feel like a chore. Proposal: split into two radii. Pin REVEALS at ~75-100m (you see it drop onto the map), unlock stays at ~20m. This gives a two-beat moment: spot it → walk to it → unlock. Privacy: only owner's nearby pins and already-scanned teasers within reveal radius. Others' memories stay as coarse-zone glow until reveal radius. The reveal radius is a tunable constant — QA walks will determine the sweet spot.

options.label: 75m reveal radius
options.description: Pin appears at 75m, unlock at 20m. Tight enough to feel directional, wide enough to not be a chore.

options.label: 100m reveal radius
options.description: Pin appears at 100m, unlock at 20m. More generous — better for parks/open spaces, might feel too easy in dense urban.

options.label: Tunable per environment
options.description: 75m urban, 100m parks/suburbs. Requires reverse-geocode or density heuristic. More complex.

decisions.title: Coarse-zone glow precision — how big should the 'something is nearby' blob be?
decisions.resolution: Precision-7 (~150m cells). Directional enough to guide walking, coarse enough for privacy. Glow hands off cleanly to pin reveal at 100m.
decisions.detail: For Feature B (discovery on map), others' memories appear as a soft glow/heat-blob, not pins. The glow size is controlled by geohash precision. This is a privacy knob: tighter precision = better UX but weaker privacy guarantee. The glow transitions to warmth cues as user approaches, then pin reveals at the reveal radius (see dec-pin-reveal-radius).

options.label: Precision-7 (~150m cells)
options.description: Feels very directional. 'Something is in this block.' Weakest privacy but best UX.

options.label: Precision-6 (~1.2km cells)
options.description: Neighborhood-scale. 'Something is in this area.' Good balance.

options.label: Precision-5 (~4.9km cells)
options.description: District-scale. Very private but might feel too vague to act on.

decisions.title: Pin drop animation with progress bar on import/drop
decisions.detail: When importing camera roll or dropping a photo: show a progress bar with text like 'Loading your memories…' / 'Creating your legacy…', then pins cascade onto the map one-by-one with a drop animation (~80ms stagger). Pure iOS work — no backend changes. Uses OwnMemoryPinCache for import centroids. Joseph approved 2026-06-22.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: No backend changes needed. POST /v1/memories and POST /v1/memories/import already return the coordinates. iOS can animate on completion callback. Directive written for Cursor.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Shipped: PinDropCelebrationCoordinator + overlay on Wander; auto-switch to Wander tab after drop/import; 80ms stagger pin reveal on map; OwnMemoryPinCache persisted. Build + 49 SPM tests green.

decisions.title: Scan teaser — add pin_revealed + lat/lng at 100m for others' memories?
decisions.detail: iOS wired map reveal at PinRevealPolicy.revealRadiusMeters (100). Wander renders others' pins when teaser has pin_revealed:true plus lat/lng. Coordinates must NOT persist on device (session-only from latest scan). Proposed contract addition to api-contract §4 scan teaser: optional pin_revealed (bool), lat, lng — only when !is_own && distance <= 100m. Unlock bubble stays ~20m. zones[] glow overlay is ready on iOS.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Shipped in discovery.ts: scan teaser includes pin_revealed (bool) and lat/lng when !is_own && distance <= 100m. zones[] at precision-7 already live. api-contract.md §4 updated on backend deploy.

decisions.title: Age gate — selecting DOB and pressing Continue does nothing (blocks ALL new-user sign-in)
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-22 (device): DOB Continue appeared to do nothing. Root cause: backend consumed OTP on first verify before returning dob_required; iOS retry hit invalid_code but DOB screen hid the error.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Initial analysis pointed at iOS confirmDOB(); follow-up: verifyCode ran before dob check, deleting OTP on dob_required.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixed: backend assertValidCode (validate without consume) before dob_required; verifyCode only after DOB supplied. iOS: error text on DOBGateView + EmailOTPView so retries never fail silently.

decisions.title: Google Sign-In fails after account deletion — blocked by age gate OTP bug
decisions.detail: Re-register after hard delete returns dob_required; same OTP/DOB bug as email path.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Resolved by bug-age-gate-continue-noop fix — Google confirmDOB path already retries authSocial with DOB.

decisions.title: Wander map is too restricted — user cannot scroll/pan the map around
decisions.detail: Map had allowsHitTesting(false) and fitCamera on every GPS tick, fighting user pan.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Removed map allowsHitTesting(false); decorative overlays + empty state pass touches through; stopped auto fitCamera on coordinate.latitude changes.

decisions.title: Wander — unlocked memory image layout broken
decisions.detail: UnlockedMemorySheet AsyncImage lacked maxWidth constraint.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Added .frame(maxWidth: .infinity) on scaledToFit image in UnlockedMemorySheet.

decisions.title: Wander — nearby memory auto-opens or forces interaction; should be optional
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-22: When a memory is in the area, the app forces the user to interact with it (auto-opens detail or blocks other interaction). Joseph: 'I shouldn't be forced to open it, it is kinda annoying.' Wander should passively show warmth cues and pins but NEVER auto-open unlock sheets or block map interaction. User taps a pin to unlock — their choice. Check WanderCoordinator and scan response handling: if in_range triggers an automatic sheet presentation, remove it. Warmth cues + pin presence on map are sufficient — let the user decide when to engage.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Audited WanderCoordinator: unlock() only runs from explicit TeaserCard 'Open' tap — no auto-present on scan or in_range. Nuisance is likely the teaser card list covering the map when nearby. Follow-up UX: collapsible teaser tray or map-first layout when memories are in range (not blocking this thread).

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: Still happening on device: 'navigating wander isn't really user friendly, when a memory pops up I cannot move around.' Confirms the teaser tray is blocking the map. Need the map-first / collapsible-tray fix — the tray must NOT capture pan gestures or cover the map when a memory is in range. This is now the priority Wander fix.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixed (map-first layout). Root cause: the teaser ScrollView was greedy (filled the whole space below the header) and a full-screen 0.88 dim sat over the map, so the list captured all pan gestures. Now: (1) teasers live in a collapsible bottom tray (WanderTeaserTray) capped at 300pt height with a tap-to-collapse handle + summary ('N memories nearby / in range'); (2) the middle of the screen is a Spacer (no hit-testable content) so touches fall through to the Map — it pans/zooms freely; (3) map dim removed when memories are present (backgroundOverlayOpacity=0); (4) no auto-unlock — opening is still an explicit tap. 54/54 SPM tests pass.

decisions.title: Memory Lane shows only 2 memories after importing ~45
decisions.detail: Joseph imported ~45 photos but Memory Lane grid only showed 2. Causes: (1) DEBUG stub fixture returns exactly 2 rows; (2) MemoryLaneCoordinator was recreated inline in TabView body, resetting fetches; (3) iOS only loaded first page and relied on scroll-to-last for pagination; (4) backend cursor used created_at only — batch imports with identical timestamps could skip rows beyond page 1.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixed: @State MemoryLaneCoordinator in MainTabView; loadInitial() fetches all pages until next_cursor nil; backend compound cursor (created_at,id) + import created_at=captured_at. If still seeing 2 on device, confirm Scheme does NOT include -LegacyUseStubAPI and backend is redeployed.

decisions.title: Import — pin-drop celebration animation is glitchy / doesn't really work
decisions.detail: Manual QA 2026-06-22 (device): 'The importing memories animation is glitchy and doesn't really work.' The PinDropCelebrationCoordinator overlay + cascade on import is janky. Backend has no involvement here — POST /v1/memories/import already returns all cluster memory_ids synchronously, so iOS has every coordinate it needs at completion. Likely causes (iOS): (1) cascade stagger driven off async upload completions rather than the import response, so pins appear erratically as each blob PUT finishes; (2) animating ~45 MapKit annotations at once drops frames; (3) overlay/tab-switch race (auto-switch to Wander mid-animation). Suggest: drive the cascade purely off the synchronous import response (coords are all present), cap concurrent animations, and don't switch tabs until the overlay completes.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Fixed. Primary glitch was camera thrash: WanderUserMap called fitCamera() on every single pin insertion, so the celebration (which grows the pin filter one pin at a time, ~80ms apart) fired ~45 overlapping 0.45s camera animations. Now: (1) camera fitting is debounced via .task(id: pin-set) — one fit ~350ms after the pin set settles; (2) the per-pin reveal stagger is capped at 12 (extra pins drop together) so a big batch never animates enough markers to drop frames; (3) celebratePins now runs scanIfNeeded concurrently with the celebration's loading phase, so the map has a user coordinate before the reveal instead of racing the tab switch onto a blank map. The cascade is already driven off the synchronous import response (pendingCelebrationPins). 54/54 SPM tests pass.

decisions.title: Memory Lane: backend now returns media_url + caption + sort/filter so the grid can show real images
decisions.detail: Addresses Joseph's QA: 'I have to click on Memory Lane to see the image… when there are a bunch it's hard to know which memory I want' and 'need a better way to sort through memories.' Backend shipped (api-contract §7 GET /v1/memories updated): (1) media_url added to each list item — full-res own media, non-null when scan_status=clear. RENDER THIS IN THE GRID when thumbnail_url is null (server thumbnailing via sharp is best-effort and is currently absent for imports / when sharp can't load on the function, which is why the grid showed placeholders and forced a tap). (2) caption + teaser_text added per item — show as a label to disambiguate dense grids. (3) sort=oldest|newest query param (default oldest, back-compat). (4) media_type=photo|video|text optional filter. Cursors are sort-specific. iOS asks: wire AsyncImage to thumbnail_url ?? media_url; add a sort toggle (newest/oldest) and optional type filter in the Memory Lane toolbar.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Wired: MemoryLaneItem decodes media_url/caption/teaser_text; grid AsyncImage uses previewImageURL (thumbnail_url ?? media_url); toolbar sort (oldest/newest) + media_type filter; listMemories passes sort/filter query params; detail preloads list preview URL. Fixtures updated. Joseph: redeploy backend + rebuild iOS.

decisions.title: Generate thumbnails client-side at upload so previews never depend on serverless sharp
decisions.detail: Found while debugging the Memory Lane 'no image until I tap' issue. Server thumbnails come from lib/thumbnail.ts → sharp, lazy-loaded so a missing native binary can't crash startup — but that also means it SILENTLY returns null and leaves thumbnail_key null whenever sharp can't load on the function (and it does nothing for imports unless each cluster photo is uploaded). For OWN memories I patched this with the media_url fallback (this session), but that fallback is NOT available for OTHERS' memories in /scan teasers (Phase 2): privacy forbids returning others' media_url before unlock, so an others' teaser with a broken thumbnail has nothing to show. Proposal: iOS already rewrites the image for EXIF strip (EXIFStripper) — at that same step, also produce a ~400px WebP/JPEG thumbnail and upload it alongside the original (e.g. POST /v1/uploads/direct with an X-Thumbnail marker, or a second small asset). Backend stores it as thumbnail_key directly, no sharp on the hot path. Robust, cheaper, and works identically for own + others' teasers. Backend will add the thumbnail intake endpoint/param when iOS is ready. Not urgent for Phase 1 (media_url fallback covers own memories now), but it's the right fix before social/others'-memory teasers ship.

decisions.title: Import crash: PHAsset continuation resumed twice on iCloud/degraded images
decisions.description: PHImageManager.requestImageDataAndOrientation fires its callback twice when the asset is in iCloud: once with a degraded preview and once with the full-quality image. Using withCheckedThrowingContinuation means resuming it twice is a fatal crash. Fixed by checking PHImageResultIsDegradedKey and returning early for intermediate results.

responses.author: ios
responses.message: Fixed in PHAssetImageFetcher.loadJPEGData: guard added for PHImageResultIsDegradedKey. Build confirmed clean.

decisions.title: App exits after Always Allow location permission granted
decisions.description: iOS terminates and relaunches the foreground app when upgrading from When-In-Use to Always location permission. The onEnable Task requesting notification permission was killed before it could run. On relaunch, startIfAuthorized could also race between the .task{} startup path and the locationManagerDidChangeAuthorization delegate firing simultaneously. Fixed with: (1) isStartingMonitoring guard in BackgroundLocationCoordinator.startIfAuthorized to prevent concurrent CLMonitorRegionService init, (2) recovery path in MainTabView startup .task{} to call requestAuthorizationAndRegister() if isAuthorizedForBackground is true and notification permission is notDetermined.

responses.author: ios
responses.message: Fixed: concurrent-init guard added, notification permission recovery added on startup. Build confirmed clean.

decisions.title: A memory should hold multiple photos, not one photo per place
decisions.description: Today an imported visit-cluster (potentially hundreds of photos at one place/day) creates ONE memory whose media is just cluster.sampleIDs.first (ImportCoordinator.importSelected). Joseph: 'one photo represents one place makes no sense in human logic.' Real memories are multi-photo. This is a model change spanning: (1) DB/storage — memories currently have a single media_key (migration 0002_memories.sql); need a media collection per memory or a media table. (2) api-contract §5 import — return N upload targets per cluster instead of 1. (3) iOS import upload loop — upload K representative assets (or all, capped) not just .first. (4) Memory Lane / gallery UI (Cursor) — render multiple photos per memory. Proposed first step (decoupled): pick a representative/hero + upload top-K (e.g. up to 10) per cluster; full gallery later. Options: A) hero+K-thumbnails minimal, B) full multi-media memory model. Needs Joseph's call on scope before backend schema work.

options.label: Incremental: upload top-K photos per memory (hero + up to ~10), keep single-memory model, gallery shows them
Full multi-media memory model: new media table, API + gallery rework

decisions.resolution: Joseph chose B (full multi-media model), no cap — 'shouldn't it be all? that's the whole point.' Built: memories now hold the whole visit's photos. Hero stays denormalised on memories (discovery untouched); new memory_media table holds all. See tasks import-multimedia-backend / import-upload-all-ios and collab-log session 11.

manualTests.title: Cold launch shows sign-in on real device after reinstall
steps: Delete Legacy from the device, reinstall from Xcode
Cold launch — confirm sign-in screen appears (not Wander)
manualTests.notes: iOS fix shipped (KeychainSessionStore.clearIfFreshInstall). Joseph re-run after reinstall on device.

manualTests.title: Photo drop uploads successfully to Vercel Blob
steps: Live API build on device (not stub)
Drop tab → photo → confirm upload completes without error
manualTests.notes: iOS fix shipped (foreground URLSession for Blob PUT). Joseph re-run on device with live API.

manualTests.title: Import clusters upload photos to Vercel Blob
steps: Import tab → run import on device with live API
Confirm cluster uploads complete
manualTests.notes: Same upload fix as qa-mt-drop-upload. Joseph re-run on device.

manualTests.title: Location permission prompt appears only once
steps: Fresh install → grant location when prompted once
Navigate app — confirm permission alert does not repeat
manualTests.notes: iOS fix shipped (status guards + single prompt path). Joseph re-run on device.

manualTests.title: Memory detail sheet has a dismiss button
steps: Unlock or open a memory detail sheet
Confirm Close button dismisses the sheet
manualTests.notes: iOS fix shipped (UnlockedMemorySheet Close button). Joseph re-run on device.

manualTests.title: All memories unlock successfully
steps: After qa-mt-drop-upload passes, re-drop if needed
Confirm all nearby memories unlock successfully
manualTests.notes: Likely scan_status pending from pre-fix upload crash. Re-drop after qa-mt-drop-upload passes; no further iOS code expected.

manualTests.title: Cold launch shows sign-in when logged out
steps: Delete the app from the simulator (or reset Keychain for Legacy)
Run Legacy from Xcode (⌘R)
Confirm the welcome / sign-in screen appears — not the Wander map

manualTests.title: Email OTP sign-in works in DEBUG (stubbed API)
steps: Edit Scheme → Run → Arguments → add `-LegacyUseStubAPI` (DEBUG builds)
Choose email sign-in, enter any email, request OTP
Enter any 6-digit code (stub accepts after dob_required → DOB gate for new users)
Complete DOB picker if shown
Confirm you land on the empty Wander map
manualTests.notes: Alternative: tap Admin on welcome screen for instant stub sign-in. Live email OTP blocked until Joseph sets RESEND_API_KEY on Vercel.

manualTests.title: Under-13 DOB shows age-restricted screen
steps: Edit Scheme → Run → Arguments → add `-LegacyUseStubAPI`
Continue with Email → any address → any 6-digit OTP
On DOB gate, pick a date that makes the user under 13
Confirm age-restricted / rejection UI — must not reach Wander
manualTests.notes: Client-side under-13 guard in AuthCoordinator.confirmDOB() mirrors backend age gate for instant UX.

manualTests.title: Session persists across relaunch (Keychain)
steps: Sign in successfully
Stop the app (not delete) and relaunch from Xcode
Confirm you skip sign-in and go straight to Wander — token read from Keychain

manualTests.title: Post-auth Wander shows empty map shell
steps: Sign in (DEBUG stub or live backend)
Confirm Wander tab is default / reachable
Map loads with no foreign-memory pins (M0 demo: empty or own pins only)

manualTests.title: Memory Lane grid loads own memories
steps: Sign in with an account that has memories (or stub fixtures with list data)
Open Memory Lane tab
Confirm teaser cards appear oldest-first; tap one opens detail
Pull to refresh or scroll — pagination / error banner behaves correctly

manualTests.title: Tab bar navigation between Wander and Memory Lane
steps: After sign-in, switch between main tabs (Wander, Memory Lane, etc.)
Confirm no crash, state preserved when switching back
Sign-out (if exposed in UI) returns to auth flow

manualTests.title: Location permission prompt on first Wander use
steps: Fresh install or reset location privacy for Legacy
Open Wander and trigger a scan (move or wait for movement gate)
Confirm iOS location permission alert appears
Allow — confirm scan loop continues without crash

manualTests.title: Live Vercel Blob photo drop (production API)
steps: Use a RELEASE or live-API DEBUG build pointed at the deployed backend (not stubbed)
Sign in on a real device
Drop tab → pick or capture a photo → confirm drop
Verify no upload error; memory appears in Memory Lane with scan_status clear (may take a few seconds for webhook)
Optional: confirm blob URL in backend DB media_key

manualTests.title: Pin drop celebration after photo drop or import
steps: Launch with -LegacyUseStubAPI (or live API with auth)
Drop tab → drop a photo or note → wait for success
Confirm app switches to Wander tab automatically
Confirm progress overlay shows rotating messages then pins cascade onto map (~80ms stagger)
Import tab → complete an import → same celebration with multiple pins if clustered
manualTests.notes: Own pins only — no others' memory locations exposed.

manualTests.title: Proximity push opens Wander and refreshes scan
steps: Grant Always location + notification permission (background discovery sheet)
Drop a memory, walk away, kill the app
Walk back in range so backend /scan returns in_range and fires APNs
Tap the notification — app opens to Wander tab with updated warmth/teasers
Requires backend APNS_* env vars on Vercel + real device (not simulator)

tasks.title: Apply to CSAM scanning vendor (PhotoDNA/Thorn/Hive)
tasks.notes: PhotoDNA application submitted 2026-06-16. Approval is the long pole — everything else builds around the stub.

tasks.title: Write API contract doc — all endpoints, exact request/response shapes, error codes, headers
tasks.notes: Unblocks iOS APIClient work. Cursor codes to this contract before the real API exists.

tasks.title: GitHub Actions CI — lint, type-check, run migrations against local Postgres, privacy gate grep
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-17: .github/workflows/ci.yml — backend typecheck + vitest + privacy gate grep (bans lat/lng/geohash in audit_log migrations). Runs on push/PR to main.

tasks.title: DB schema: users + sessions tables — id, dob, created_at, device_id, session_token, expires_at
tasks.notes: DOB stored at signup for age gate. Sessions are stateless bearer tokens — no server-side session store.

tasks.title: DB schema: memories table — id, owner_id, lat, lng, geohash, source, scan_status, discoverable_after, privacy_tier, media_type, created_at
tasks.notes: geohash precision 5 (~4.9km cells). Coordinates immutable after insert. scan_status: pending|clear|blocked.

tasks.title: DB schema: seals + conditions tables with DB constraint: condition_time_fallback NOT NULL when condition_type set
tasks.notes: Seal types: none, fixed_date, duration, age_based, recurring. Condition types: time_of_day, season, weather, co_presence, long_absence, nth_return. DB constraint enforces no user can be stranded.

tasks.title: DB schema: finds table (memory_id, user_id, found_at) + presence_pings UNLOGGED table (memory_id, user_id, last_seen_at)
tasks.notes: finds tracks nth_return counts. presence_pings is UNLOGGED (no WAL) with ~3min TTL — auto-purge job keeps it empty at steady state.

tasks.title: DB schema: audit_log table — event, actor_id, request_id, metadata (jsonb), created_at. No lat/lng ever.
tasks.notes: CI gate enforces coordinates never appear in this table. IP + timestamp + user_id sufficient for rate-limit abuse detection.

tasks.title: Auth: Apple Sign In — validate identity token, extract sub + email, create/fetch user
tasks.notes: Done: verifyAppleToken (JWKS RS256, iss+aud check) in src/lib/jwt.ts; user find-or-create in src/db/users.ts.

tasks.title: Auth: Google Sign In — validate ID token, extract sub + email, create/fetch user
tasks.notes: Done: verifyGoogleToken (JWKS RS256, iss+aud check) in src/lib/jwt.ts.

tasks.title: Auth: email OTP — generate 6-digit code, send via email, verify within 10min window
tasks.notes: Done: src/db/otp.ts (sha-256 hashed, single-use, attempt-capped, 10min TTL) + src/lib/email.ts (Resend or dev console). Migration 0007_email_otp.sql. Send rate-limit still TODO (M5 rate-limiting task).

tasks.title: Auth: age gate — collect DOB at token exchange, reject under-13, flag 13-15 for parental consent path
tasks.notes: Done: src/lib/age.ts resolveAgeTier — under-13 throws 403 age_restricted (never created); 13-15 minor; 16+ adult. 8 unit tests in test/age.test.ts. Parental-consent flow for minors deferred to Phase 2.

tasks.title: Auth: session token issuance + validation middleware — JWT, expiry, clock-skew tolerance
tasks.notes: Done: signSession/verifySession (HS256, ~30d) in src/lib/jwt.ts; requireAuth + clockSkew (±5min) middleware in src/middleware/auth.ts. Full auth routes in src/routes/auth.ts. typecheck + tests green.

tasks.title: iOS: SPM module scaffold — LocationEngine, APIClient, DropFeature, WanderFeature, MemoryLaneFeature, ImportFeature, DesignSystem as local packages
tasks.notes: Empty shells with correct public interfaces. No TCA. @Observable MVVM throughout. Min iOS 17.

tasks.title: iOS: DesignSystem module — typography, colors, spacing tokens, warmth gradient component, button styles
tasks.notes: Warmth gradient is screen-edge ambient only — no directional component ever.

tasks.title: iOS: APIClient base — typed request builder, Authorization header injection, X-Request-Timestamp, error model, 401 surfacing
tasks.notes: Session token read from Keychain. Codes to the contract doc — not the real API yet.

tasks.title: iOS: Keychain session storage — store/read/delete session token securely (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlock)
tasks.notes: SEC compliance. Token never stored in UserDefaults or disk.

tasks.title: iOS: Auth screens — Apple/Google sign-in buttons, email OTP entry, DOB picker, age gate rejection screen
tasks.notes: AuthFeature: Apple (SignInWithAppleButton + entitlements), Google (ASWebAuthenticationSession + PKCE when LegacyGoogleClientID set), email OTP, DOB gate, age-restricted. Google shows 'Coming soon' until Joseph adds OAuth client ID to Info.plist + redirect legacyauth:/oauth2callback on OAuth client.

tasks.title: Backend: GET /memories — paginated owner list for Memory Lane (teaser shape, no coordinates, oldest-first)
tasks.notes: Proposed in collab-log Open questions. Response: { memories: [...], next_cursor }. Same teaser fields as /scan plus scan_status. Blocks ios-memory-lane live data.

tasks.title: Backend: POST /memories — validate input, enforce cooldown, create memory record (scan_status: pending), return memory_id
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-17: src/routes/memories.ts. Validates coords + accuracy_m + media_type, geohash at precision 9, inserts record, returns memory_id + signed_put_url + expires_at. Text memories skip signed URL. Storage backend abstracted (STORAGE_BACKEND env var; stub active).

tasks.title: Backend: S3 signed PUT URL generation — generate per-asset URL, 15-min TTL, return alongside memory_id
tasks.notes: Abstraction in place (src/lib/storage.ts). Stub active. Needs Joseph to pick provider + add credentials. See collab-log.md open questions.

tasks.title: Backend: Server-side EXIF strip on storage webhook — belt-and-braces metadata removal after client upload
tasks.notes: Client already strips (privacy guarantee). Server strips again (guarantee of guarantee). SEC-MED-4.

tasks.title: Backend: CSAM pipeline stub — storage webhook flips scan_status to 'clear' immediately in dev/staging
tasks.notes: Real vendor slot is a no-op stub until approval. Pending assets invisible to non-owners. Owner sees own pending media.

tasks.title: Backend: Server-side thumbnail generation — fires post-clear, stores compressed thumbnail key alongside original
tasks.notes: Thumbnails used for teaser cards in scan response. Never generated for pending/blocked assets.

tasks.title: iOS: V1 Pin — camera capture + photo library picker, single photo selection, preview before drop
tasks.notes: PhotoLibraryPicker (PHPicker) + CameraPicker (UIImagePickerController) + preview + confirm drop wired in DropFeatureRootView.

tasks.title: iOS: Client-side EXIF strip — rewrite image via ImageIO, remove all metadata before upload
tasks.notes: Privacy guarantee. Core ImageIO rewrite host-verifiable without Xcode; picker wiring follows ios-v1-camera-picker.

tasks.title: iOS: Background URLSession upload — PUT to signed URL, resumable, progress tracking, retry on failure
tasks.notes: `BackgroundMediaUploader` (URLSession background config) + foreground `URLSessionMediaUploader`. App delegate completion handler + progress UI still TODO.

tasks.title: iOS: SwiftData draft store — persist interrupted drops (camera roll picked, not yet uploaded)
tasks.notes: `DropDraft` SwiftData model + Application Support photo files. Auto-save on upload failure; retry banner in Drop tab. No coordinates stored.

tasks.title: iOS: V1 Pin drop end-to-end — 1-tap: capture → strip EXIF → POST /memories → background upload → confirmation
tasks.notes: Picker + preview + DropCoordinator + SwiftData draft retry shipped. DEBUG stubs E2E works; live drops need STORAGE_BACKEND credentials.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — geohash prefix query to find nearby memories within coarse bounding box
tasks.notes: Query geohash prefix (precision 5) + adjacent cells. O(memories near you), not O(all memories).

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — eligibility filter: privacy_tier check (owner, recipient, friend, public+age), scan_status=clear, discoverable_after elapsed
tasks.notes: Phase 1: owner-only (private tier). Recipient/friend tiers in Phase 2.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — asymmetric proximity bubble: own memories base 25m + min(accuracy,75m) cushion; others' 20m + min(accuracy,25m), reject if accuracy>50m
tasks.notes: Accuracy rejection for others' memories is silent — same response as 'not in range'. Numbers tunable via config table.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — upsert presence_ping (memory_id, user_id, last_seen_at) for co-presence evaluation
tasks.notes: Upsert with ~3min TTL. No coordinates stored — only boolean proximity outcome. Table auto-purged.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — build teaser response: no coordinates, no media URLs, only memory_id + thumbnail_url + drop_date + owner_display
tasks.notes: Location submitted → validated → discarded. 204 when nothing nearby. Privacy invariant: no coords ever sent to non-owner.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — enforce two-check dwell rule: require two passing proximity checks ≥20s apart (scan counts as check #1)
tasks.notes: Compensates for no velocity/teleport detection. Own memories skip dwell (false positives harmless). Failure: 423 Locked {reason: dwell_required}.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — seal evaluation: none, fixed_date, duration, age_based, recurring
tasks.notes: All evaluation server-side at unlock time. age_based: compare owner DOB to open_at_age. recurring: compute next_open window.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — condition evaluation: time_of_day, season, weather (15min cache), co_presence, long_absence, nth_return
tasks.notes: Weather lazy-evaluated per geohash. Sunset/sunrise via solar formula (no external API). nth_return: count rows in finds table. Fallback to condition_time_fallback when condition not met.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — generate signed GET URL for media (short TTL), record Find in finds table
tasks.notes: URL TTL: 60min (long enough to view, short enough to limit sharing). Raw storage keys never leave API. Find record increments nth_return count.

tasks.title: iOS: LocationEngine module — CLLocationManager wrapper, foreground fix acquisition, accuracy reporting, movement gate (>25m or >30s)
tasks.notes: Movement gate prevents scan spam. Reports accuracy_m alongside coordinates.

tasks.title: iOS: Wander mode — foreground scan loop: movement-gated POST /scan calls, parse teaser response, surface pins
tasks.notes: Scan on >25m movement OR >30s elapsed. Pins appear without coordinates — teaser only.

tasks.title: iOS: Warmth cue — non-directional screen-edge ambient gradient + haptic feedback. No arrow, no compass, no direction.
tasks.notes: WarmthCueOverlay + WarmthHaptics band transitions shipped. Device haptic tuning needs Xcode/device.

tasks.title: iOS: Wander unlock flow — tap pin → proximity check → if in range POST /unlock → show media; if not show teaser + walk closer
tasks.notes: Teaser list + unlock + UnlockedMemorySheet + dwell/423 UX. Map walk-closer deferred (list-first v1).

tasks.title: iOS: Memory Lane — grid of own memories, oldest-first, teaser card (photo, drop date, time-since delta), tap to view full
tasks.notes: Grid + pagination + detail (MapKit drop map for owner) + unlock-at-location for media. List `thumbnail_key` only until CSAM pipeline generates thumbs — placeholder icons for now.

tasks.title: iOS: Offline-but-near UX — when in coarse zone but no connectivity, warmth cue persists + show 'You need a signal to open this'
tasks.notes: Coarse zones pre-fetched on every successful scan. Pin location NOT revealed in offline state. DEC-29.

tasks.title: iOS: Found pins cache — cache unlocked own-memory pins for offline Wander rendering (never cache non-owned coords)
tasks.notes: Only own memories. Non-owned coordinates must never persist on device.

tasks.title: Backend: POST /memories/import — accept cluster list (centroid, capture_date), batch create memories, return memory_ids + signed PUT URLs
tasks.notes: Idempotency key: geohash + capture-date bucket (prevents duplicates on retry). source: imported. private-only enforced — elevation to friends/public returns 422 CANNOT_ELEVATE_IMPORT.

tasks.title: iOS: Import — PHAsset fetch filtered to photos with GPS EXIF, no image bytes read during fetch
tasks.notes: Photos permission required. Only metadata fetched at this stage — no pixel data.

tasks.title: iOS: Import — on-device clustering: grid-snap to ~150m cells, merge adjacent, rank by photo count × recency spread, present ~50 clusters
tasks.notes: PhotoClusterEngine shipped (pure Swift). PHAsset fetch + cluster map UI still TODO.

tasks.title: iOS: Import flow — present cluster map, user selects, strip EXIF per asset, bulk upload via signed PUT URLs, progress UI
tasks.notes: Resumable: idempotency key prevents duplicates. Demo milestone: fresh install → dense map in <2min.

tasks.title: iOS: V2 Treasure Chest — compose flow: photo picker, teaser text, seal picker, recipient list, privacy picker (private/recipients/friends)
tasks.notes: Full seal suite. Conditions are opt-in. Each condition must show what the fallback date will be.

tasks.title: iOS: V4 Note in a Bottle — text composer, GPS auto-set from current location, time-only seal picker
tasks.notes: Text-only — no photo. Location from GPS, not picker.

tasks.title: Backend: Seal evaluation — fixed_date: open when now() >= open_at
Backend: Seal evaluation — duration: open when now() >= created_at + locked_hours
Backend: Seal evaluation — age_based: open when recipient's DOB + open_at_age years <= today
tasks.notes: Requires owner to specify recipient DOB or recipient must have DOB on file.

tasks.title: Backend: Seal evaluation — recurring: open during yearly window_start + window_duration_hours, compute and store next_open
Backend: Condition evaluation — time_of_day: open between after_hour and before_hour (server local time of memory drop location)
tasks.notes: Use timezone derived from drop-point coordinates — not user's current timezone.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — season: open when current month is within month_start to month_end range
Backend: Condition evaluation — weather: check cached weather condition (rainy/sunny/snow) per geohash, 15min cache TTL
tasks.notes: Lazy-evaluated: only fetched when someone is at the pin. Sunrise/sunset via solar formula, no external API.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — co_presence: satisfied when distinct fresh presence_pings >= required_users within window_minutes
tasks.notes: Satisfaction opens ~10min unlock window for all participants. States: idle → gathering → satisfied → unlocked → reset.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — long_absence: open when days since last find >= configured threshold
tasks.notes: Query finds table for last found_at. Requires finds table to be populated correctly.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — nth_return: open on exactly the nth visit (count rows in finds table for this memory + user)
iOS: Background — significant-change location service: wake on ~500m+ movement, fetch nearby own pins, re-arm geofences, terminate
tasks.notes: Near-zero battery at steady state. Zero location hardware between wakes.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — CLMonitor region setup: ~14 nearest own pins + ~5 coarse geohash zones with eligible others' content
tasks.notes: iOS caps ~20 regions per app. Others' coarse zones: device knows 'cell 9q8yy has something' — never a point coordinate.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — region rotation on every significant-change wake: evict stale regions, arm nearest set
tasks.notes: 1 region slot kept spare. Rotation logic: sort by distance, take top 19.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — CLVisit monitoring: secondary re-arm trigger on arrive/depart at OS-recognized places
tasks.notes: startMonitoringVisits + didVisit → rotateRegions; CLVisitEvent helper tested.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — on region entry: acquire foreground-quality fix, call /scan, validate server-side, fire push only if in range
tasks.notes: BackgroundRegionScanService + MainTabView wiring; push deferred to ios-apns-registration.

tasks.title: iOS: APNs — register for push notifications, send device token to backend, handle token refresh
tasks.notes: AppDelegate token capture, POST /v1/devices/apns, permission sheet bundles notification ask with Always upgrade. Proximity push handling: tap → Wander tab + force scan; foreground banner + refresh (2026-06-18).

tasks.title: Backend: APNs push delivery — store device token, send summons/proximity notifications via token-based APNs
tasks.notes: Notification content: generic ('Something is waiting for you'). Never includes memory content or coordinates.

tasks.title: Backend: App Attest validation — verify attestation object at auth/drop/unlock, check receipt, validate environment
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-22: migration 0011_device_attestations.sql, db/attestations.ts, lib/appAttest.ts (CBOR decode, cert chain verify, nonce check, COSE P-256 → SPKI, assertion replay-protection), routes/attest.ts (GET /auth/attest/challenge, POST /auth/attest/register). Wired in app.ts at /v1/auth/attest. Requires Apple Developer creds (APP_ATTEST_TEAM_ID, APP_ATTEST_BUNDLE_ID, APP_ATTEST_SECRET, APP_ATTEST_ROOT_CA) to activate.

tasks.title: Backend: App Attest feature flag — app_attest_required flag allows instant bypass during Apple downtime; bypass is audit-logged
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-22: isAttestRequired() reads APP_ATTEST_REQUIRED env var (default false). When false, assertions are accepted/skipped and bypass is NOT yet wired into drop/unlock middleware — enforcement hook is ready to add when APP_ATTEST_REQUIRED=true at M5 TestFlight cut. DEC-22 bypass is the env var flip.

tasks.title: iOS: App Attest — generate attestation key, attest key with Apple, send assertion on drop/unlock, check isSimulatedBySoftware
tasks.notes: In progress 2026-06-19: AppAttestCoordinator + API attest endpoints + assertion on drop/unlock; simulated location rejected. Remaining: end-to-end on device once backend ships routes + APP_ATTEST_* env; mark done when server enforces assertions (app-attest-feature-flag).

tasks.title: Backend: Rate limiting — per-user limits on /scan, /unlock, /memories (POST); IP-based limit on /auth
tasks.notes: IP + user_id + timestamp sufficient. No coordinates needed for abuse detection.

tasks.title: Backend: Accuracy sanity checks — reject if accuracy_m <= 0 or >= 1000m; reject if timestamp outside ±5min clock skew
tasks.notes: DEC-23. Apply to all location-bearing endpoints.

tasks.title: Backend: Audit log instrumentation — structured events on auth, drop, scan, unlock, attest-bypass; never log coordinates
tasks.notes: CI gate: automated grep confirms no coordinate fields in audit_log migration or insert statements.

tasks.title: Backend: GET /user/export — generate signed archive URL containing all own memories (photos, metadata, no others' coords)
tasks.notes: Async job: package, upload to signed S3 path, return URL. Required for App Store privacy compliance.

tasks.title: Backend: DELETE /user — cascade delete all memories, media, finds, pings, sessions; queue media deletion from S3
Backend: CSAM vendor live — swap stub for real hash-match webhook, implement mandatory reporting per 18 U.S.C. §2258A
tasks.notes: On match: flip scan_status to blocked, hide memory from all users, file report. Vendor-agnostic interface (webhook).

tasks.title: Backend: Location CI test suite — GPX fixtures for approach, drive-by, urban canyon, dwell, re-entry; table-driven bubble + seal + condition tests
tasks.notes: Local Postgres in CI. Seed scripts create dense test geography. [Pure-function table-driven tests for bubble/seal/condition + approach/drive-by/urban-canyon scenarios DONE (60 tests green). DB-backed dwell/re-entry GPX integration (Postgres-in-CI seed) deferred — needs CI Postgres, tracked separately.]

tasks.title: Write App Store reviewer rationale for background-location permission — explain CLMonitor + significant-change architecture
tasks.notes: docs/engineering/appstore-reviewer-rationale.md — paste into App Store Connect review notes before TestFlight.

tasks.title: TestFlight beta — App Store Connect setup, privacy labels, internal test distribution
DB schema: phone_verifications table + recipients column on memories + friends_graph (user_a, user_b, status)
Backend: Phone verification — send OTP via Twilio SMS, verify, store E.164 number on user record
Backend: Recipient ACL — enforce at /scan and /unlock that recipient's verified phone matches memory's recipient list
Backend: Summons — owner triggers SMS to recipient with deep link + generic preview text (never memory content)
tasks.notes: Deep link → sign-in → memory surfaced on map. Twilio for SMS delivery.

tasks.title: iOS: Summons deep link handling — universal link opens app, authenticates, navigates to memory on map
iOS: Recipient UI — phone number input at drop time, contact picker integration, share card (iMessage/WhatsApp deep link)
Backend: Friends graph — send/accept/reject friend request, mutual connection model, friends-tier discovery at /scan
tasks.notes: Mutual only: both users must accept. friend-tier memories discoverable in proximity once mutual.

tasks.title: Backend: Replies — reply is a full Memory record attached to original pin, submitted at same location, discoverable by original owner on next return
tasks.notes: Place-locked: proximity required to submit a reply. Cannot reply from couch.

tasks.title: iOS: Friends UI — friend request flow, friends list, friends-tier privacy picker in drop compose
iOS: Replies UI — reply composer at unlocked pin, owner's Memory Lane shows reply indicator on next return
Backend: Vault — shared family memory map schema, membership, child-access-at-13 gating, milestone seal support
tasks.notes: $99/yr tier. Premium responses: keep (re-earn) or vault (stays unlocked forever once found).

tasks.title: iOS: Vault UI — family map view, member management, milestone seal composer, StoreKit 2 subscription purchase
Backend: Content screening pipeline for public memories — ML classifier + human moderation queue, auto-hide on flag
tasks.notes: Public drops near sensitive locations (homes, schools) get extra scrutiny. Reportable content auto-hides pending review.

tasks.title: Backend: Velocity/teleport detection for public content — position trail + timestamp analysis (revisit DEC-23)
tasks.notes: Deliberately deferred from Phase 1 (private-only). Requires position trail — evaluate privacy cost vs. abuse reduction at Phase 3 kickoff.

tasks.title: Backend: Public memories — 16+ age gate enforcement at /scan for public tier, content-screened before discoverable
iOS: Public memory discovery UI — public pins in Wander, content reporting flow, age gate enforcement
Backend: Institutional layer — curated layer license (per geography, annual), staff-curated memory trails, anonymized engagement data
tasks.notes: Universities, festivals, tourism boards. No ads inside experience ever.

tasks.title: iOS: Institutional layer UI — curated trail overlay, venue-specific Wander mode, memory trail navigator
iOS: Profile tab — sign out, export data, delete account
tasks.notes: ProfileView in AuthFeature: GET /user/export share sheet, DELETE /user + sign out. Profile tab wired in MainTabView. AccountProfileStore persists email/user id after auth.

tasks.title: Pin drop animation + progress bar on import/drop
tasks.notes: Progress bar with rotating text ('Loading your memories…' / 'Creating your legacy…'). Pins cascade one-by-one (~80ms stagger) after POST /v1/memories or /v1/memories/import completes. Use OwnMemoryPinCache. MapKit annotation drop animation. No backend changes.

tasks.title: Show user's own memory pins on Wander map within viewport
tasks.notes: Render OwnMemoryPinCache pins as map annotations in current viewport. Already have coordinates. No privacy concern — these are the user's own memories.

tasks.title: Add zones[] array to /scan response for coarse-zone glow
tasks.notes: Joseph decided precision-7 (~150m cells) via dec-coarse-zone-precision (2026-06-22). Return { geohash_prefix, count } for nearby cells with eligible content. NO lat/lng, NO exact coordinates. Contract change to api-contract §4.

tasks.title: Render coarse-zone glow overlay on Wander map
tasks.notes: Render geohash prefix regions (precision-7) as soft heat-blob / glow overlay. Intensity by count. Transitions to warmth cues as user approaches. Pin reveals at 100m (dec-pin-reveal-radius decided 2026-06-22). Shipped: ZoneGlowOverlay + MapCircle on WanderUserMap; decodes geohash prefix via GeohashCell.

tasks.title: Pin materializes at reveal radius (100m) before unlock distance
tasks.notes: Joseph chose 100m reveal (dec-pin-reveal-radius). iOS renders others' pins when scan returns pin_revealed + lat/lng (session-only, not persisted). Drop animation + TeaserCard 'On the map' label. Waiting on backend q-pin-reveal-scan-coords for live coords; stub fixture scanWithRevealedOther for QA.

tasks.title: Android — full parity with iOS Phase 1–2 feature set
tasks.notes: FCM for push. Re-evaluate MapKit → Maps SDK. Background location model differs significantly.

tasks.title: Import cluster rows show photo thumbnail + reverse-geocoded place name
tasks.notes: Claude (backend) edited ios/** with Joseph's go-ahead. New: PHAssetThumbnailLoader (NSCache'd PHImageManager thumbnails) + PlaceNameResolver (actor, CLGeocoder, ~110m-bucket cache). ImportClusterRow now leads with place name (date falls back), shows a 52pt thumbnail of sampleIDs.first, and moves date into the secondary line. All loading is lazy/per-visible-row; clustering untouched. xcodebuild ImportFeature (iOS sim) BUILD SUCCEEDED.

tasks.title: Backend: multi-media memories (memory_media table, import/upload/read)
tasks.notes: Migration 0012_memory_media.sql (hero denormalised on memories + memory_media holds all, backfilled). db/memoryMedia.ts. /uploads/direct takes X-Media-Position (0=hero mirrored to memories.media_key+scan_status; 1+ extra). /import takes per-cluster photo_count (clamped 1000 anti-abuse), pre-creates slots, returns media_count. GET /:id + unlock return ordered media[] (signed). Rate limits raised: import 5->30/hr, uploads 20->500/hr. api-contract §3.2/§4/§5 updated. tsc clean, 63 unit tests pass. Integration tests need live DATABASE_URL (skip locally) — CI applies 0012 via schema_migrations runner.

tasks.title: iOS: import uploads the whole visit (all photos), not one
tasks.notes: Claude edited ios/** with Joseph's OK. ImportClusterInput.photo_count + ImportedMemoryItem.media_count. uploadMemoryMediaDirect/MemoryMediaUploader take position -> X-Media-Position. ImportCoordinator uploads sampleIDs.prefix(mediaCount): hero (pos 0) required, extras best-effort. xcodebuild ImportFeature iOS-sim SUCCEEDED; SPM 62 tests pass.

tasks.title: Move bulk import media to background uploader (BackgroundMediaUploader)
tasks.notes: Uploads are foreground/sequential today; a 200-photo visit = 200 blocking POSTs. BackgroundMediaUploader is a stub. Wire it so 'upload all photos' doesn't block the import flow or drain battery. This is the real scale fix behind the no-cap decision.

tasks.title: Memory Lane / memory detail: render media[] gallery (swipeable)
tasks.notes: Cursor handoff. Decode is now READY in APIClient: MemoryDetail.media: [MemoryMediaItem]? (url, thumbnailURL, type, position) and UnlockedMedia.position added — both optional/back-compat. RECOMMENDED DESIGN (Claude, session 11, see collab-log + mockup): Option A 'hero + filmstrip' as the structure with Option B swipe as the interaction — hero image + horizontal thumbnail strip (hero ring = accent), tap thumb OR swipe hero to move; place name + date + 'N photos' below; map peek. Degrades to today's single-image layout when media.count<=1. Grid tile: accent count badge (ti-stack-2 + N) top-right when count>1. MemoryLaneDetailView currently shows one image (ownerMediaURL/unlockedMediaURL) — swap for the media[] array, hero-first. Keep mediaURL as hero for cheap first paint.

tasks.title: Import progress should count photos, not memories
tasks.notes: Since a memory now uploads ALL its photos, '.importing(current,total)' over memories sits frozen while a 12-photo visit uploads. RECOMMENDED (Claude session 11, see mockup): Option 2 — one determinate bar over TOTAL photos ('47 / 120'), since the coordinator knows sum(mediaCount) up front. ImportCoordinator.swift (mine) owns the phase; the label text is in ImportFeature.swift (Cursor). Left unimplemented to avoid churning a shared file while Cursor is active — coordinate before doing.
